Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 1, 021 [SB Anzu 2][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
Column i
o i 1o i 1

⸢bi₂?-riq?⸣ [x x] ⸢šu-ku-nu⸣ a-dan-nu

(Belet-ili saying to Ninurta:) 'Flash [...], fix the appointed time,

o i 22

[a]-⸢na⸣ DINGIR-⸢MEŠ⸣ šu-⸢ut ab?-nu?⸣-u ⸢na-mir⸣-tu₂ šu-⸢ṣi⸣

'send out light for the gods whom I created!

o i 33

gu-⸢um-mur-ta de⸣-[ke] ⸢qa-bal⸣-ka

'Raise your entire battle,

o i 44

⸢šub-riq im⸣-ḫul-[li-ka] ⸢lil-li-ku e⸣-li-⸢šu⸣

'make lightning strike, let your destructive winds go against him!

o i 55

mu-⸢up⸣-par-šu₂ an-[za-a] ⸢ku⸣-mu-⸢ma⸣

'Conquer winged Anzu,

o i 66

⸢ri-ḫi⸣-iṣ er-[ṣe-ta ib]-⸢ba⸣-nu-u šu-bat ⸢su-uḫ⸣-ḫi

'devastate the earth where he was created, wreck (his) dwelling!

o i 77

⸢ap-luḫ-tu⸣ [li-ir-ta]-⸢ṣi-na⸣ e-li-šu

'Let (your) battle-array resound against him,

o i 88

⸢na⸣-an-⸢dur-tu⸣ qa-⸢bal⸣-[ka] ⸢li⸣-ru-ub-šu

'let your ferocious battle become furious against him.

o i 99

⸢lip⸣-[tar?]-⸢rik-šu⸣ gu-⸢um-mur-ta a-šam⸣-šu-⸢ut⸣-tu₄

'Let an entire dust storm keep blocking him,

o i 1010

tu*-lul giš⸢PAN šu-ku⸣-du-ka ⸢im-ta li⸣-me-⸢šu⸣1

'draw the bow, let your arrow become poison to him.

o i 1111

gal-la-niš ⸢liš⸣-[ta-an]-⸢nu⸣-u bu-nu-⸢ka⸣

'Let your features be changed like a gallû-demon,

o i 1212

⸢šu-ṣi im⸣-[ba-ra] ⸢zi?⸣-[me-ka] ⸢a-a⸣ u₂-⸢ad-di⸣

'send out a fog, so that he cannot distinguish your face.

o i 1313

[bir]-⸢bir-ru⸣-[ka] lil-li-ku e-li-šu

'Let your brilliance go against him,

o i 1414

⸢šiḫ-ṭu⸣-[ka] ⸢lu⸣-u₂ ṣi-ru ša₂-⸢lum-ma⸣-tu₂ lu ti-šu

'let your attack be glorious, may you have refulgence.

o i 1515

[a]-a ip-⸢pu-ḫa⸣ dUTU ⸢e-li⸣-šu

'Let the Sun not shine on him,

o i 1616

[u₄]-⸢mu⸣ nam-ru ⸢a-na da-ʾu⸣-um-ma-ti ⸢li⸣-tur-šu

'let the bright day turn to darkness for him.

o i 1717

⸢šu⸣-[ri]-⸢iḫ nap-šat⸣-su ⸢an⸣-za-a ku-⸢mu-ma⸣

'Destroy his life, conquer Anzu!

o i 1818

ša₂-a-⸢ri⸣ [x x x] ⸢a-na⸣ [bu]-⸢su-ra?-te li-ib⸣-[lu-ni]

'Let the winds bring [...] for news,

o i 1919

bi-⸢tuš E₂⸣.[KUR a]-⸢na⸣ ṣe-⸢er a⸣-bi-ka dEN.LIL₂

'to the temple Ekur, to your father Ellil.

o i 2020

[ša₂]-⸢de-e⸣ [qer]-⸢bet⸣-su-⸢nu ri⸣-ḫi-iṣ ⸢dul⸣-liḫ₃-ma

'Devastate (and) trouble the mountain meadows,

o i 2121

lem-na [...] i-ki-is nap-šat-su

'cut the throat of evil [...]!

o i 2222

⸢šar-ru⸣-[tu] ⸢li⸣-ru-⸢bu⸣ a-na E₂.KUR

'Let kingship (re-)enter Ekur,

o i 2323

[a]-⸢na⸣ a-[bi a]-li-di-⸢ka⸣ li-⸢tu⸣-ru par-ṣi

'let the divine powers return to the father, your parent.

o i 2424

[li]-⸢ib⸣-[šu]-⸢ma⸣ lib-ba-nu-u pa-rak-ki

'Let cult daises exist and be built,

o i 2525

[...] ⸢er⸣-bet-ti [ši]-⸢tak⸣-ka-na ma-ḫa-zu-ka

'[...] four [...] keep setting up your shrines!

o i 2626

[ma-ḫa-zu]-⸢ka⸣ [li]-ru-⸢bu⸣ a-na E₂.KUR

'Let your shrines enter Ekur,

o i 2727

[...] ⸢maḫ⸣-[ri] DINGIR-MEŠ-⸢ma? ga-aš₂⸣-ru lu MU-ka

'[...] before the gods, let strong one be your name.'

o i 2828

[x x um-mi]-⸢šu iš⸣-[me]-⸢e⸣ qu-ra-du

The hero heard his mother's [...],

o i 2929

[... ša₂]-⸢du?-us⸣-su ig-guš

[...], he went to his mountain.

o i 3030

[x x uš-te-eṣ]-⸢bi⸣-ta ⸢se⸣-bet qab-la

[...] hitched up the seven battles,

o i 3131

[x x uš-te]-⸢eṣ⸣-bi!(ŠE-)⸢ta⸣ se-bet ⸢im-ḫul-li⸣

[...] hitched up the seven destructive winds.

o i 3232

[...] ⸢tu⸣-qu-⸢un-tu ig⸣-ru

[...], he launched combat,

o i 3333

[... i-qu]-⸢lu ziq⸣-ziq-qu

[...] the counterblasts attended.

o i 3434

[...] ⸢it-ta-an-ma-ru⸣

[...] they (Anzu and Ninurta) met,

o i 3535

[... i-ru]-⸢ba⸣-aš₂-šu

[...] he (Anzu) became furious with him.

o i 3636

[... KUR]⸢e⸣ ik-⸢tum₃⸣

[...], he covered the mountain [...],

o i 3737

[...] ⸢uz-zu⸣

[...] by anger.

o i 3838

[... qar-ra]-⸢du⸣

[...] the hero (Anzu saying to Ninurta):

o i 3939

[... ka-li-šu₂]-⸢nu⸣ par-⸢ṣi⸣

'[...] all the divine powers,

o i 4040

[...] ⸢um-te!(MU-)er!(UM)

'[...] I have instructed.

o i 4141

[...] ⸢ṭe₄⸣-en-ka ⸢SUM⸣

'[...] Give your reason!'

Column ii
o ii 1o ii 1

u₂-[... zi]-⸢kir pi-šu₂⸣

... [...] the utterance of his mouth [...],

o ii 22

an-⸢za⸣-[a ... qu]-⸢ra-du d⸣NIN.⸢URTA⸣

the hero Ninurta [...] Anzu (saying):

o ii 33

a-⸢na⸣-[ku ... d]⸢DUR⸣.AN.⸢KI⸣

'I [...] Duranki (Ellil),

o ii 44

mu-⸢kin₂⸣ [x (x)] x [x (x)] x x d⸢60 šar₄⸣ NAM-MEŠ

'confirmer of [...] ... [...] ..., of Ea, king of destinies.

o ii 55

a-na ⸢e?⸣-[pe?]-⸢šu?⸣ ME₃-ka al-li-ka ⸢ad-iš-ka⸣

'I have come to do battle with you, I have trampled you down.'

o ii 66

iš-me-⸢ma⸣ an-zu-u₂ zi-kir pi-i-šu

Anzu heard the utterance of his mouth,

o ii 77

ina ⸢qe₂⸣-reb ḫur-sa-a-ni ik-kil-la-šu ⸢šam⸣-riš ⸢id⸣-di

in the middle of the mountains furiously he voiced his clamour.

o ii 88

šak-⸢na⸣-at!(LA) ek-le-tu₂ KURu₂ ⸢pa-nu-šu? kat₃⸣-mu

Darkness had fallen, the face of the mountain was shrouded,

o ii 99

⸢dUTU⸣ nu-ur₂ DINGIR-MEŠ da-um-miš₂ i-ru-⸢up⸣2

the Sun, the light of the gods, grew dark in pitch blackness.

o ii 1010

x x (x) BU-ti ⸢ut⸣-ta-ʾa-ar it-ti an-⸢zi⸣-[i ri-gim]-šu₂3

... he was roaring, the sign of Anzu was his voice,

o ii 1111

ina bi-rit <qab>-⸢li? tuq⸣-[ma]-a-te a-na-⸢an-tu₂ it⸣-[ku]-pu  ; [...]

amidst the battle, the combats, war was very close, [...].

o ii 1212

⸢u₂-rat ap-luḫ⸣-[tu] ⸢da⸣-[me] ⸢i-ram?⸣-[muk]

The battle-array being aroused, it was bathing in blood,

o ii 1313

⸢er-pe-et⸣ mu-ti [i-za]-⸢an⸣-nu-nu ⸢i⸣-bar-⸢riq⸣ [uṣ]-⸢ṣi⸣

a cloud of death was raining down, flashing arrows,

o ii 1414

⸢iš⸣-ta-⸢uṣ ina bi⸣-ri-šu₂-nu i-⸢ram-mu-um⸣ qab-lu

battle rushed between them, roaring.

o ii 1515

ga-aš₂-ru ⸢šu-pu-u⸣ bu-kur₂ d⸢ma-mi⸣

The strong one, the resplendent one, son of Mami,

o ii 1616

⸢tuk-lat d⸣a-nu₃ u dda-⸢gan⸣ [x x]  ; dNIN.ŠI.[KU₃]4

trust of Anu and Dagan, [...] of Niššiku (Ea),

o ii 1717

[it]-⸢lul* gišPAN qa⸣-na-a ⸢u₂⸣-[mal-li]-šu₂5

drew the bow, he nocked a reed arrow on it,

o ii 1818

[...] ⸢qa?-na⸣-[a] u₂-⸢bil⸣-[šum-mu]

[...] he launched a reed arrow against him.

o ii 1919

⸢ul iṭ⸣-[ḫe] ⸢a-na⸣ an-zi-i qa-[nu]-u  ; it-tu-⸢ra⸣

The reed arrow did not approach Anzu (but) returned,

o ii 2020

an-⸢zu⸣-[u₂-um]-⸢ma is⸣-ša₂-a e-⸢li-šu⸣

Anzu shouted against it:

o ii 2121

qa-nu-⸢um⸣-[ma ...] a;[pu-uk]-⸢ka⸣

'Reed arrow [...] to your reed-bed,

o ii 2222

mu-⸢um⸣-[mu ...]

'frame [...],

o ii 2323

⸢šer₂-a⸣-[nu ...]

'sinew [...]!'

(rest of column missing)
Column iii
o iii 1o iii 1

[... ub]-⸢bu-ḫa!⸣ [e]-⸢pir⸣ KARAŠ6

(Ninurta instructing Šarur to say to Ea:) '"[...] he (Ninurta) was enveloped by the dust of devastation.

Reverse
Column i
(start of column missing)
r i 1'r 1'

⸢ub?-lu⸣ [...]  ; [...]

"[...] launched [...].

r i 2'2'

uš-ḫa-[ri-ir-ma ...]

"[...] fell silent, [...],

r i 3'3'

⸢it⸣-ta-⸢ab⸣-[ṭa-lu-ma ...]  ; [...]

"[...] stopped, [...]."'

r i 4'4'

⸢dŠAR₂*⸣.UR₄* ⸢uš⸣-[kin ...]7

Šarur prostrated himself, [...],

r i 5'5'

ši-pir ta-⸢ḫa⸣-[zi ... d]e₂-[a d]⸢NIN.ŠI⸣.KU₃

[...] the battle message [...] Ea, Niššiku.

r i 6'6'

mim-mu-u₂ [...] ⸢a⸣-na d⸢e₂⸣-a u₂-ša₂-an-⸢ni⸣

All that [...] he repeated to Ea:

r i 7'7'

⸢be⸣-lu₄-um-⸢ma⸣ [...] ⸢la!⸣-me-ma KI.MIN8

'The lord (Ninurta) [...] had surrounded [...] - Ditto.'

r i 8'8'

⸢iš⸣-me-⸢ma d⸣[NIN.ŠI].⸢KU₃⸣ a-mat DUMU-šu₂

Niššiku heard the words of his son,

r i 9'9'

x [...] ⸢a⸣-ma-ti u₂-ma-ʾi-⸢ir?⸣-šu₂

... [...], he gave him instructions:

r i 10'10'

x [...] x ana be-⸢li?⸣-ka a-mat zik-ri pi-ia

'... [...] ... for your lord the words my mouth utters,

r i 11'11'

mim-[mu]-⸢u₂ a?⸣-dab-bu-bu u₂-ṣur a-na ⸢ša₂⸣-šu₂

'all that I say, heed for him:

r i 12'12'

⸢qab⸣-[lu]-um-ma a-a i-nu-uḫ šu-kun lit-ka

"Let battle not rest, bring about your victory,

r i 13'13'

šu-ni-⸢iḫ⸣-šu-ma ina mit-ḫur me-ḫe-e a-par-šu₂ lid-di

"tire him out with opposing the storm wind, so that he lets his wing tip fall!

r i 14'14'

le-⸢qe₂⸣-e-ma be-lu₄ ana ar-⸢ka⸣kat₃ ⸢šu⸣-ku-di-ka9

"Take, O lord, your arrows towards the back,

r i 15'15'

nu-⸢uk-kis⸣ ap-re-e-šu im-na u šu-⸢me⸣-la* ru-ʾi-im10

"cut off his wing tips, strike (them) off on the right and the left!

r i 16'16'

liṭ-⸢ṭul⸣-ma kap-pa-a-šu₂ ⸢lit⸣-ba-la zi-kir pi-⸢i⸣-šu₂

"Let him look and let his wings take away the utterance of his mouth,

r i 17'17'

kap-[pa x] kap-pi i-šas-si i tap-⸢laḫ₃-šu₂⸣

"he will shout: 'Wing [...] wing!' - you should not fear him.

r i 18'18'

⸢tu?⸣-[lul] ina i-rat ⸢giš?PAN⸣-ka lil-li-ku  ; qa-nu-u₂ bir-qi₂

"Draw from the breast of your bow, so that reed arrows of lightning fly

r i 19'19'

⸢ap-ru⸣ kap-pi ki kur-da-me lim-mel-lu11

"(and) the tips of the wings play like a butterfly!

r i 20'20'

⸢šu⸣-ri-iḫ nap-šat-su an-za-a ku-mu-ma

"Destroy his life, conquer Anzu!

r i 21'21'

ša₂-⸢a⸣-ri kap-pi-šu₂ a-na bu-us-ra-te lib-⸢lu-ni⸣

"Let the winds bring his wings for news,

r i 22'22'

bi-[tuš] ⸢E₂⸣.KUR a-na ṣe-ri a-bi-⸢ka dEN⸣.LIL₂

"to the temple Ekur, to your father Ellil.

r i 23'23'

⸢KURi⸣ qer-be₂-su-nu ri-ḫi-iṣ dul-liḫ-ma

"Devastate (and) trouble the mountain meadows,

r i 24'24'

[lem]-⸢na⸣ an-za-a i-ki-is nap-šat-su

"cut the throat of evil Anzu!

Column ii
r ii 1r ii 1

[...] ⸢E₂⸣.KUR

"[...] Ekur,

r ii 22

[...] par-⸢ṣi⸣

"[...] the divine powers [...].

r ii 33

[... lib-ba]-⸢nu⸣-[u] ⸢pa⸣-rak-⸢ki⸣

"[...] let cult daises be built,

r ii 44

[... ši-tak]-⸢ka?-na⸣ [ma]-⸢ḫa?⸣-zu!(LI-)[ka]

"[...] keep setting up your shrines!

r ii 55

[... li-ru-bu]-⸢ma?⸣ a-⸢na⸣ [E₂].KUR

"Let [...] enter Ekur,

r ii 66

[... DINGIR-MEŠ]-⸢ma?⸣ [ga-aš₂]-⸢ru lu MU-ka⸣

"[...] the gods, let strong one be your name."'

r ii 77

[...] ⸢ter-ta⸣

[...] the instruction,

r ii 88

[... it-ba]-la a-⸢na dNIN!.URTA!⸣

[...] he (Šarur) took away to Ninurta.

r ii 99

[... id-bu]-⸢bu⸣ u₂-ša₂-⸢na a⸣-na ša₂-šu₂

[...] he (Ea) said he repeated to him:

r ii 1010

[... i-nu]-⸢uḫ? šu-kun lit-ka⸣

'[...] rest, bring about your victory,

r ii 1111

[... i]-⸢na mit⸣-[ḫur] ⸢me-ḫe⸣-e a-par-šu₂ ⸢lid⸣-[di]

'[...] with opposing the storm wind, so that he lets his wing tip fall!

r ii 1212

[... be]-⸢lu₄ ana⸣ [...] ⸢šu?-ku⸣-di-⸢ka⸣

'[...], O lord, your arrows towards [...],

r ii 1313

[...] x [(x)] ⸢ŠU.BI⸣.AŠ?.⸢AM₃⸣

'[...] ... [(...)] Ditto.'

r ii 1414

[iš-me]-⸢ma be-lu₄⸣ [x x] ⸢de₂⸣-a ⸢dNIN⸣.ŠI.KU₃

The lord heard [...] of Ea Niššiku,

r ii 1515

[iq]-ru-ur ⸢ir-ta⸣-ʾu-ub ša₂-⸢du⸣-us-su ⸢ig⸣-guš

he quivered, he became furious, he went to his mountain.

r ii 1616

⸢be⸣-[lu₄] ⸢uš⸣-te-eṣ-bi-ta ⸢se⸣-bet-⸢tu₄? qab⸣-li

The lord hitched up the seven battles,

r ii 1717

qar-⸢ra-du uš⸣-te-eṣ-⸢bi-ta se⸣-bet-⸢tu₄?⸣ IM.ḪUL

the hero hitched up the seven destructive winds,

r ii 1818

⸢mu-um-mil-lat⸣ ep-⸢ri KI?⸣ x a-⸢šam⸣-šu-tu₂

the one that stirs up the dust ... the dust storm.

(colophon)
r ii 1919

⸢GIM⸣ SUMUN-šu₂ SAR-ma ba-⸢a⸣-ri

Written and checked according to its original.

r ii 2020

[...] ⸢150? MU? ŠID?-BI?⸣.IM

[...] its line-count is 150.

r ii 2121

[...] x [...]12

[...] ... [...]

(1 line blank)
r ii 2222

⸢iti⸣NE ⸢U₄ 25⸣-KAM

Abu, 25th day,

r ii 2323

[lim]-⸢mu!(NUMUN) [m]NUMUN—⸢DU₃ lu₂⸣ša₂-kin₃ ra-ṣa-pi

eponym: Zera-ibni, governor of Raṣappa.

(rest of column blank)

1for collation see O.R. Gurney, AfO 28 (1981-82), 92

2or i-ru-ub# 'set'

3or it-ti 'with'

4possible erased sign after {d#} in {d#}a-nu₃

5for collation see O.R. Gurney, AfO 28 (1981-82), 92

6this line is written vertically between columns o i and o ii

7for collation see H.W.F. Saggs, AfO 33 (1986), 16

8KI#.MIN signals a repetition of Ninurta's message in Tablet II 72-85

9the copy does not support ar-ka#-at₂

10for collation see Vogelzang 1988, p. 87

11a reading qur₂-da-me, 'enemy' or 'enemies' might be worth considering

12either blank line or lost short line

Created by Frances Reynolds for the AHRC-funded GKAB Project, 2008 and released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/cams/gkab/P338338
